name: Boston Cream Pie
recipeIngredient: 1 Cup Half and Half, hot (not boiling)
1 Tablespoon Butter, cut into small pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. To make the cake:
a. Grease two 8-inch round layer cake pans and line the bottoms with parchment or waxed paper, greasing again, then sprinkling with flour to coat completely. Set aside.
b. Sift together flour, baking powder, and salt three times and set aside.
c. In a large bowl, beat eggs for 3-4 minutes until thick and lemon colored. Gradually add sugar and beat for another 5 minutes (or 8 minutes by hand). Add vanilla and mix well.
d. Melt butter in hot milk using a rubber spatula. Mix the hot milk into the batter gradually, folding in the flour at the same time. The mixing should take about 1 minute. The batter will be thin. Divide between prepared pans and bake for 20-25 minutes until a cake tester inserted in the center comes out clean. Cool on wire racks for about 10 minutes. Remove cakes from pans and continue to cool.
e. Spread pastry cream between cake layers, then spread chocolate glaze over the top layer. Allow glaze to set for 2 hours before serving.
3. To make the cream filling:
a. In a heavy-bottomed saucepan, combine sugar, flour, and salt. Gradually add hot milk while stirring with a wire whisk until no lumps remain.
b. Cook over medium-high heat, stirring constantly until bubbly. Cook for 2 minutes until it begins to thicken. Remove from heat and stir in egg yolks, beating well. Return mixture to saucepan and cook for 2 more minutes until thick and smooth. Add vanilla and stir in butter. Cover surface with plastic wrap and chill in refrigerator.
4. To make the chocolate glaze:
a. In a small saucepan, combine chocolate chips, milk, and butter over very low heat until melted and smooth. Gradually mix in confectioners sugar, stirring in vanilla. Cool until thick enough to spread.
Yield: 8 servings
